links.title: Parameter Importance is Not Static: Evolving Parameter Isolation for Supervised Fine-Tuning

primary_link.title: Parameter Importance is Not Static: Evolving Parameter Isolation for Supervised Fine-Tuning

papers.summary: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) of large language models often suffers from task interference and catastrophic forgetting. Recent approaches alleviate this issue by isolating task-critical parameters during training. However, these methods represent a static solution to a dynamic problem, assuming that parameter importance remains fixed once identified. In this work, we empirically demonstrate that parameter importance exhibits temporal drift over the course of training. To address this, we propose
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型的 Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) 經常遭受任務干擾和 catastrophic forgetting 的困擾。最近的方法通過在訓練期間隔離任務關鍵參數來緩解這個問題。然而，這些方法代表了一個動態問題的靜態解決方案，假設參數重要性一旦確定就保持不變。在這項工作中，我們通過實證證明參數重要性在訓練過程中呈現出時間漂移。為了解決這個問題，我們提出
papers.title: Parameter Importance is Not Static: Evolving Parameter Isolation for Supervised Fine-Tuning
papers.title_zh: 參數重要性並非靜態: 用於 Supervised Fine-Tuning 的演進式參數隔離

links.title: Causal Drawbridges: Characterizing Gradient Blocking of Syntactic Islands in Transformer LMs

primary_link.title: Causal Drawbridges: Characterizing Gradient Blocking of Syntactic Islands in Transformer LMs

papers.summary: We show how causal interventions in Transformer models provide insights into English syntax by focusing on a long-standing challenge for syntactic theory: syntactic islands. Extraction from coordinated verb phrases is often degraded, yet acceptability varies gradiently with lexical content (e.g., "I know what he hates art and loves" vs. "I know what he looked down and saw"). We show that modern Transformer language models replicate human judgments across this gradient. Using causal interventions
papers.summary_zh: 我們展示了 Transformer 模型中的 causal interventions 如何透過關注句法理論的一個長期挑戰：syntactic islands，來提供對英語句法的見解。從 coordinated verb phrases 中提取（資訊）通常會導致語法退化，然而其可接受度卻隨著詞彙內容（例如，「I know what he hates art and loves」與「I know what he looked down and saw」）呈現梯度變化。我們展示了現代 Transformer 語言模型能在此梯度上重現人類的判斷。利用 causal interventions
papers.title: Causal Drawbridges: Characterizing Gradient Blocking of Syntactic Islands in Transformer LMs
papers.title_zh: Causal Drawbridges：探討 Transformer LMs 中句法孤島的梯度阻斷

primary_link.title: DiPO: Disentangled Perplexity Policy Optimization for Fine-grained Exploration-Exploitation Trade-Off

papers.summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has catalyzed significant advances in the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, effectively managing the exploration and exploitation trade-off remains a critical challenge. In this paper, we fully analyze the exploration and exploitation dilemma of extremely hard and easy samples during the training and propose a new fine-grained trade-off mechanism. Concretely, we introduce a perplexity space disentangling strateg
papers.summary_zh: 帶有可驗證獎勵的強化學習 (Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards, RLVR) 促進了大型語言模型 (Large Language Models, LLMs) 推理能力的顯著進步。然而，有效管理探索與利用的權衡仍然是一個關鍵挑戰。在本文中，我們全面分析了訓練過程中極難和極易樣本的探索與利用困境，並提出了一種新的細粒度權衡機制。具體來說，我們引入了一個 perplexity space disentangling strateg
papers.title: DiPO: Disentangled Perplexity Policy Optimization for Fine-grained Exploration-Exploitation Trade-Off

links.title: SparseBalance: Load-Balanced Long Context Training with Dynamic Sparse Attention

primary_link.title: SparseBalance: Load-Balanced Long Context Training with Dynamic Sparse Attention

papers.summary: While sparse attention mitigates the computational bottleneck of long-context LLM training, its distributed training process exhibits extreme heterogeneity in both \textit{1)} sequence length and \textit{2)} sparsity sensitivity, leading to a severe imbalance problem and sub-optimal model accuracy. Existing algorithms and training frameworks typically focus on single issue, failing to systematically co-optimize these two problems. Therefore, we propose SparseBalance, a novel algorithm-system co-
papers.summary_zh: 儘管 sparse attention 緩解了 long-context LLM 訓練的計算瓶頸，但其分散式訓練過程在 1) 序列長度 (sequence length) 和 2) 稀疏性敏感度 (sparsity sensitivity) 方面都表現出極端異質性，導致嚴重的負載不平衡問題和次優的模型準確性。現有演算法和訓練框架通常只關注單一問題，未能系統性地共同最佳化這兩個問題。因此，我們提出了 SparseBalance，這是一種新穎的演算法-系統協同

papers.title_zh: SparseBalance：帶有動態稀疏注意力的負載均衡長上下文訓練

Whether models whose visual representations more closely mirror human neural processing are also more resistant to adversarial pressure is an open question with implications for both neuroscience and AI safety. papers.title: Gaslight, Gatekeep, V1-V3: Early Visual Cortex Alignment Shields Vision-Language Models from Sycophantic Manipulation
papers.title_zh: Gaslight, Gatekeep, V1-V3：早期視覺皮層對齊保護視覺語言模型免受逢迎操縱

links.title: ToolOmni: Enabling Open-World Tool Use via Agentic learning with Proactive Retrieval and Grounded Execution

primary_link.title: ToolOmni: Enabling Open-World Tool Use via Agentic learning with Proactive Retrieval and Grounded Execution

papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) enhance their problem-solving capability by utilizing external tools. However, in open-world scenarios with massive and evolving tool repositories, existing methods relying on static embedding retrieval or parameter memorization of tools struggle to align user intent with tool semantics or generalize to unseen tools, respectively, leading to suboptimal accuracy of open-world tool retrieval and execution. To address these, we present ToolOmni, a unified agentic framew
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (Large Language Models, LLMs) 透過利用外部工具來增強其問題解決能力。然而，在具有大量且不斷演進的工具儲存庫的開放世界場景中，依賴靜態 embedding retrieval 或工具參數記憶的現有方法難以將使用者意圖與工具語義對齊，也難以推廣到未見過的工具，分別導致開放世界工具檢索和執行的次優準確性。為了應對這些挑戰，我們提出了 ToolOmni，這是一個統一的 Agentic framew

papers.title_zh: ToolOmni：透過結合主動檢索和 Grounded Execution 的 Agentic 學習實現開放世界工具使用

links.title: From Anchors to Supervision: Memory-Graph Guided Corpus-Free Unlearning for Large Language Models

primary_link.title: From Anchors to Supervision: Memory-Graph Guided Corpus-Free Unlearning for Large Language Models

papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) may memorize sensitive or copyrighted content, raising significant privacy and legal concerns. While machine unlearning has emerged as a potential remedy, prevailing paradigms rely on user-provided forget sets, making unlearning requests difficult to audit and exposing systems to secondary leakage and malicious abuse. We propose MAGE, a Memory-grAph Guided Erasure framework for user-minimized, corpus-free unlearning. Given only a lightweight user anchor that identifi
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 可能會記住敏感或受版權保護的內容，引發嚴重的隱私和法律問題。儘管機器遺忘 (machine unlearning) 已成為一種潛在的補救措施，但主流範式依賴於用戶提供的遺忘集 (forget sets)，這使得遺忘請求難以審計，並使系統面臨二次洩漏和惡意濫用的風險。我們提出了 MAGE，這是一個記憶圖譜引導的擦除 (Memory-grAph Guided Erasure) 框架，用於實現用戶最小化、無語料庫的遺忘。只需提供一個輕量級的用戶錨點 (user anchor) 即可識別
papers.title: From Anchors to Supervision: Memory-Graph Guided Corpus-Free Unlearning for Large Language Models
papers.title_zh: 從錨點到監督：基於記憶圖譜引導的無語料庫大型語言模型遺忘

links.title: Weight Patching: Toward Source-Level Mechanistic Localization in LLMs

primary_link.title: Weight Patching: Toward Source-Level Mechanistic Localization in LLMs

papers.summary: Mechanistic interpretability seeks to localize model behavior to the internal components that causally realize it. Prior work has advanced activation-space localization and causal tracing, but modules that appear important in activation space may merely aggregate or amplify upstream signals rather than encode the target capability in their own parameters. To address this gap, we propose Weight Patching, a parameter-space intervention method for source-oriented analysis in paired same-architectur
papers.summary_zh: 機械性可解釋性 (Mechanistic interpretability) 旨在將模型行為定位到因果實現其的內部組件。先前的工作推進了激活空間定位 (activation-space localization) 和因果追蹤 (causal tracing)，但在激活空間中看似重要的模組，可能只是聚合或放大上游信號，而非在其自身的參數中編碼目標能力。為了解決這一差距，我們提出了 Weight Patching，這是一種參數空間干預方法，用於配對相同架構中的源導向分析
papers.title: Weight Patching: Toward Source-Level Mechanistic Localization in LLMs
papers.title_zh: Weight Patching：邁向 LLMs 中源級機械性定位

primary_link.title: Ordinary Least Squares is a Special Case of Transformer

papers.summary: The statistical essence of the Transformer architecture has long remained elusive: Is it a universal approximator, or a neural network version of known computational algorithms? Through rigorous algebraic proof, we show that the latter better describes Transformer's basic nature: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) is a special case of the single-layer Linear Transformer. papers.summary_zh: Transformer 架構的統計本質長期以來一直難以捉摸：它是一個通用逼近器 (universal approximator)，還是已知計算演算法的神經網路版本？通過嚴格的代數證明，我們表明後者更能描述 Transformer 的基本性質：Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) 是單層 Linear Transformer 的一個特例。利用經驗共變異數矩陣的譜分解，我們構建了一個特定的參數設置，其中注意力機制

papers.title_zh: Ordinary Least Squares 是 Transformer 的一個特例

links.title: (How) Learning Rates Regulate Catastrophic Overtraining

primary_link.title: (How) Learning Rates Regulate Catastrophic Overtraining

papers.summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is a common first stage of LLM post-training, teaching the model to follow instructions and shaping its behavior as a helpful assistant. At the same time, SFT may harm the fundamental capabilities of an LLM, particularly after long pretraining: a phenomenon known as catastrophic overtraining (Springer et al., 2025). To understand overtraining, we first investigate catastrophic forgetting in finetuning through the lens of implicit regularization of the learning rate.
papers.summary_zh: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) 是 LLM 後訓練的常見第一階段，它教會模型遵循指令並將其行為塑造成一個有用的助手。同時，SFT 可能會損害 LLM 的基本能力，尤其是在長時間預訓練之後：這種現象被稱為災難性過度訓練 (catastrophic overtraining) (Springer et al., 2025)。為了理解過度訓練，我們首先透過學習率的隱式正則化視角，研究 fine-tuning 中的災難性遺忘 (catastrophic forgetting)。
papers.title: (How) Learning Rates Regulate Catastrophic Overtraining
papers.title_zh: 學習率如何調節災難性過度訓練

primary_link.title: One Token per Highly Selective Frame: Towards Extreme Compression for Long Video Understanding

With each video frame typically expanding into tens or hundreds of tokens, the limited context length of large language models (LLMs) forces the VLMs to perceive the frames sparsely and lose temporal information. papers.summary_zh: 對於 vision-language models (VLMs) 來說，長視訊理解本質上具有挑戰性，因為影格數量龐大。每個視訊影格通常會擴展成數十或數百個 token，而大型語言模型 (LLMs) 有限的上下文長度迫使 VLMs 稀疏地感知影格並丟失時間資訊。為了解決這個問題，我們探索在最終 LLM 層實現每個影格一個 token 的極致視訊 token 壓縮。我們的關鍵見解是啟發式

links.title: LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

primary_link.title: LongCoT: Benchmarking Long-Horizon Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

papers.summary: As language models are increasingly deployed for complex autonomous tasks, their ability to reason accurately over longer horizons becomes critical. An essential component of this ability is planning and managing a long, complex chain-of-thought (CoT). We introduce LongCoT, a scalable benchmark of 2,500 expert-designed problems spanning chemistry, mathematics, computer science, chess, and logic to isolate and directly measure the long-horizon CoT reasoning capabilities of frontier models. Proble
papers.summary_zh: 隨著 language models 越來越多地部署於複雜的 autonomous tasks，其在更長的時間範圍內準確推理的能力變得至關重要。這種能力的一個基本組成部分是規劃和管理一個長而複雜的 chain-of-thought (CoT)。我們引入了 LongCoT，這是一個可擴展的 benchmark，包含 2,500 個專家設計的問題，涵蓋 chemistry、mathematics、computer science、chess 和 logic，旨在分離並直接測量 frontier models 的長程 CoT 推理能力。

links.title: POINTS-Seeker: Towards Training a Multimodal Agentic Search Model from Scratch

primary_link.title: POINTS-Seeker: Towards Training a Multimodal Agentic Search Model from Scratch

papers.summary: While Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) demonstrate impressive visual perception, they remain epistemically constrained by their static parametric knowledge. To transcend these boundaries, multimodal search models have been adopted to actively interact with the external environment for evidence retrieval. Diverging from prevailing paradigms that merely retrofit general LMMs with search tools as modular extensions, we explore the potential of building a multimodal agentic search model from scratch.
papers.summary_zh: 儘管 Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) 展示了令人印象深刻的 visual perception 能力，但它們的認知能力仍然受到其靜態 parametric knowledge 的限制。為了超越這些界限，multimodal search models 已被採用來主動與 external environment 互動以檢索 evidence。我們有別於目前僅將 search tools 作為 modular extensions retrofitting 到一般 LMMs 上的主流 paradigms，探索了從零開始構建 multimodal agentic search model 的潛力。
papers.title: POINTS-Seeker: Towards Training a Multimodal Agentic Search Model from Scratch
papers.title_zh: POINTS-Seeker: 邁向從零開始訓練 Multimodal Agentic Search Model

papers.summary: Memory-based self-evolution has emerged as a promising paradigm for coding agents. However, existing approaches typically restrict memory utilization to homogeneous task domains, failing to leverage the shared infrastructural foundations, such as runtime environments and programming languages, that exist across diverse real-world coding problems. papers.summary_zh: 基於記憶的 self-evolution 已成為 coding agents 的一個有前景的 paradigm。然而，現有方法通常將 memory utilization 限制在 homogeneous task domains，未能利用存在於多樣真實世界 coding problems 中的共享 infrastructural foundations，例如 runtime environments 和 programming languages。為了解決這個限制，我們透過利用來自 heterogeneous domains 的統一 memory pool，研究了 Memory Transfer Learning (MTL)。
papers.title: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
papers.title_zh: Memory Transfer Learning: 記憶如何在 Coding Agents 中跨領域轉移

links.title: How Can We Synthesize High-Quality Pretraining Data? A Systematic Study of Prompt Design, Generator Model, and Source Data

primary_link.title: How Can We Synthesize High-Quality Pretraining Data? A Systematic Study of Prompt Design, Generator Model, and Source Data

papers.summary: Synthetic data is a standard component in training large language models, yet systematic comparisons across design dimensions, including rephrasing strategy, generator model, and source data, remain absent. We conduct extensive controlled experiments, generating over one trillion tokens, to identify critical factors in rephrasing web text into synthetic pretraining data. Our results reveal that structured output formats, such as tables, math problems, FAQs, and tutorials, consistently outperform
papers.summary_zh: Synthetic data 是訓練 large language models 的標準組成部分，然而，跨設計維度（包括 rephrasing strategy、generator model 和 source data）的系統性比較仍然缺乏。我們進行了廣泛的 controlled experiments，生成了超過一兆個 tokens，以識別將 web text 重述為 synthetic pretraining data 的關鍵因素。我們的結果顯示，structured output formats，例如 tables、math problems、FAQs 和 tutorials，始終表現優異。

links.title: Robust Reward Modeling for Large Language Models via Causal Decomposition

primary_link.title: Robust Reward Modeling for Large Language Models via Causal Decomposition

papers.summary: Reward models are central to aligning large language models, yet they often overfit to spurious cues such as response length and overly agreeable tone. Most prior work weakens these cues directly by penalizing or controlling specific artifacts, but it does not explicitly encourage the model to ground preferences in the prompt's intent. We learn a decoder that maps a candidate answer to the latent intent embedding of the input. The reconstruction error is used as a signal to regularize the reward
papers.summary_zh: Reward models 對於對齊 large language models 至關重要，然而它們經常 overfit 於 spurious cues，例如 response length 和過於順從的 tone。大多數先前的工作透過 penalizing 或 controlling specific artifacts 直接削弱這些 cues，但沒有明確鼓勵 model 將 preferences 建立在 prompt's intent 上。我們學習了一個 decoder，它將 candidate answer 映射到輸入的 latent intent embedding。Reconstruction error 被用作一個 signal 來 regularize reward。
papers.title: Robust Reward Modeling for Large Language Models via Causal Decomposition
papers.title_zh: 透過 Causal Decomposition 對 Large Language Models 進行穩健的 Reward Modeling

primary_link.title: UI-Copilot: Advancing Long-Horizon GUI Automation via Tool-Integrated Policy Optimization

papers.summary: MLLM-based GUI agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in complex user interface interaction tasks. However, long-horizon scenarios remain challenging, as these agents are burdened with tasks beyond their intrinsic capabilities, suffering from memory degradation, progress confusion, and math hallucination. To address these challenges, we present UI-Copilot, a collaborative framework where the GUI agent focuses on task execution while a lightweight copilot provides on-demand assistance for m
papers.summary_zh: 基於 MLLM 的 GUI 代理已在複雜的使用者介面互動任務中展現出強大能力。然而，長序列場景仍具挑戰性，因為這些代理負擔了超出其內在能力的任務，面臨記憶力衰退、進度混淆和數學幻覺等問題。為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了 UI-Copilot，這是一個協作框架，其中 GUI 代理專注於任務執行，而一個輕量級 copilot 則提供隨需協助。

papers.title_zh: UI-Copilot: 透過工具整合的策略優化推進長序列 GUI 自動化

links.title: Robust Ultra Low-Bit Post-Training Quantization via Stable Diagonal Curvature Estimate

primary_link.title: Robust Ultra Low-Bit Post-Training Quantization via Stable Diagonal Curvature Estimate

papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are widely used across many domains, but their scale makes deployment challenging. Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) reduces memory footprint without retraining by leveraging a small calibration set. Recent Hessian-based PTQ methods compensate quantization error via cross-channel dependencies, but such approaches degrade at low bit-widths due to noisy curvature estimates from limited calibration data. We propose DASH-Q, a robust PTQ framework using diagonal Hessian ap
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 被廣泛應用於許多領域，但其規模使得部署極具挑戰性。Post-Training Quantization (PTQ) 透過利用一小部分校準集來減少記憶體佔用，而無需再訓練。最近基於 Hessian 的 PTQ 方法透過跨通道依賴性補償量化誤差，但由於來自有限校準數據的雜訊曲率估計，此類方法在低位元寬度時會退化。我們提出了 DASH-Q，這是一個使用對角 Hessian 近似的穩健 PTQ 框架。

links.title: QuantileMark: A Message-Symmetric Multi-bit Watermark for LLMs

primary_link.title: QuantileMark: A Message-Symmetric Multi-bit Watermark for LLMs
